title: SpaceX enters the Nasdaq 100 and receives bullish recommendations from investment banks
summary: SpaceX joins the Nasdaq 100 index and several Wall Street banks issue optimistic forecasts as it prepares for its initial public offering.

title: Global stock markets fall due to semiconductor slump and concerns about AI spending
summary: The Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq close lower, dragged down by losses in chip stocks like Samsung, while the German Dax breaks its record streak and the Spanish Ibex also retreats.

title: Samsung's record earnings fail to boost Asian and European markets
summary: Despite reporting a historic profit, the drop in Samsung's shares drags down Asian and European stock exchanges, amid concerns about semiconductor demand.

title: Trump rings the Wall Street bell from the White House and the Dow Jones hits a new high
summary: President Donald Trump symbolically participates in the opening of the New York market just as the Dow Jones reaches a closing record.

title: Financial regulators warn about the increase in cyberattacks using artificial intelligence
summary: The ECB and other banking supervisors demand that banks have an action plan in the face of the growing threat of AI-driven cyberattacks.

title: Roadside assistance faces a driverless future
summary: The expansion of autonomous vehicles could make traditional roadside assistance services obsolete, according to analysts.

title: Cognizant Technology Solutions leads S&P 500 gains with a 6% rise
summary: Cognizant shares surge after the IT services company reported quarterly results that exceeded expectations.

title: Oil price rises after revocation of Iranian crude export licenses
summary: The United States cancels oil export permits to Iran, pushing Brent up more than 3% in international markets.

title: Amazon plans a $25 billion bond issuance
summary: The e-commerce giant seeks to raise funds through the largest corporate debt issuance of the year to finance investments.

title: Best Buy and Apple warn of an impact on consumer prices
summary: Retail chains warn that the increase in the cost of electronic components will be passed on to final prices.

title: Rivian places 75 million shares in a new public offering
summary: The electric vehicle manufacturer seeks to raise additional capital in a competitive market.

title: Bank of England plans to ease capital rules for banks
summary: The British monetary authority proposes reducing capital requirements to stimulate credit and economic growth.

title: China grants investors greater access to bonds in Hong Kong
summary: The Asian country expands foreign participation in the Hong Kong bond market, as a step toward financial opening.

title: Greek shipping companies earned nearly $4 billion transporting Russian oil
summary: Greek-flagged ships have moved Russian crude over the past three years, obtaining high profits despite sanctions.

title: Big audit firms face regulatory troubles in Australia
summary: The Big Four firms are having difficulties with Australian authorities due to accounting and compliance scandals.

title: NATO to invest 40 billion euros in anti-drone systems
summary: Allied countries commit to allocating a historic amount to drone defense, given the evolution of aerial threats.

title: Rheinmetall and Lockheed Martin agree to build a missile factory in Europe
summary: The German and American defense companies will create a joint plant to manufacture missiles and strengthen European military capability.

title: Norfolk Southern and Union Pacific defend their $85 billion merger
summary: The two major US railroad companies argue that the deal will improve efficiency and service before regulators.

title: Canada to invest up to 400 million Canadian dollars in Teck Resources
summary: The Canadian government supports critical metals production with a strategic investment in miner Teck.

title: A K2 Airways cargo plane loses contact after a technical failure
summary: A Boeing 737 cargo plane of the Pakistani airline disappears from radar after reporting a technical problem; a search is underway.
